title: Myths
puzzle: Why do myths survive even when people know they are not literal history?
insight: Myths endure because they organize fear, hope, identity, and explanation into memorable stories that communities can share across generations.
key_ideas: Myths often explain origins, danger, or order.
They carry values as much as facts.
A myth can stay useful even when it is not treated as literal.
mind_blower: People often keep myths not because they are naive, but because myths are efficient containers for meaning.
next_curiosity: If myths encode meaning so well, what modern stories play a similar role today?
narration_text: Why do myths survive even when people know they are not literal history? Myths endure because they organize fear, hope, identity, and explanation into memorable stories that communities can share across generations. Myths often explain origins, danger, or order. They carry values as much as facts. A myth can stay useful even when it is not treated as literal. People often keep myths not because they are naive, but because myths are efficient containers for meaning. If myths encode meaning so well, what modern stories play a similar role today?
